annotations.label: quality
annotations.definition: A dependent entity that inheres in a bearer by virtue of how the bearer is related to other entities

annotations.label: branched
annotations.definition: A branchiness quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's having branches.

annotations.label: linear
annotations.definition: A shape quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's being narrow, with the two opposite margins parallel.

annotations.label: ratio
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's magnitude in proportion to the magnitude of another entity.
annotations.comment: Examples: height to weight; brain size to body size; root to shoot ratio; urinary sodium to potassium ratio. waist-hip ratio. {terms:contributor="https://orcid.org/0000-0001-8314-2140"}

annotations.label: laminar
annotations.definition: A quality inhering in a bearer by virtue of the bearer's processing the form of a thin plate sheet or layer.

annotations.label: sequentially ordered entity
annotations.definition: Any entity that is ordered in discrete units along a linear axis.

annotations.label: sequence atomic unit
annotations.definition: Any individual unit of a collection of like units arranged in a linear order

annotations.label: sequence bearer
annotations.definition: Any entity that can be divided into parts such that each part is an atomical unit of a sequence

annotations.label: system
annotations.definition: A material entity consisting of multiple components that are causally integrated.

annotations.label: part of
annotations.definition: a core relation that holds between a part and its whole

annotations.label: has part
annotations.definition: a core relation that holds between a whole and its part

annotations.label: realized in
annotations.definition: [copied from inverse property 'realizes'] to say that b realizes c at t is to assert that there is some material entity d & b is a process which has participant d at t & c is a disposition or role of which d is bearer_of at t& the type instantiated by b is correlated with the type instantiated by c. (axiom label in BFO2 Reference: [059-003])
annotations.comment: Paraphrase of elucidation: a relation between a realizable entity and a process, where there is some material entity that is bearer of the realizable entity and participates in the process, and the realizable entity comes to be realized in the course of the process

annotations.label: realizes
annotations.definition: Paraphrase of elucidation: a relation between a process and a realizable entity, where there is some material entity that is bearer of the realizable entity and participates in the process, and the realizable entity comes to be realized in the course of the process
annotations.comment: Paraphrase of elucidation: a relation between a process and a realizable entity, where there is some material entity that is bearer of the realizable entity and participates in the process, and the realizable entity comes to be realized in the course of the process

annotations.label: preceded by
annotations.definition: x is preceded by y if and only if the time point at which y ends is before or equivalent to the time point at which x starts. Formally: x preceded by y iff ω(y) <= α(x), where α is a function that maps a process to a start point, and ω is a function that maps a process to an end point.

annotations.label: precedes
annotations.definition: x precedes y if and only if the time point at which x ends is before or equivalent to the time point at which y starts. Formally: x precedes y iff ω(x) <= α(y), where α is a function that maps a process to a start point, and ω is a function that maps a process to an end point.

annotations.label: occurs in
annotations.definition: b occurs_in c =def b is a process and c is a material entity or immaterial entity& there exists a spatiotemporal region r and b occupies_spatiotemporal_region r.& forall(t) if b exists_at t then c exists_at t & there exist spatial regions s and s’ where & b spatially_projects_onto s at t& c is occupies_spatial_region s’ at t& s is a proper_continuant_part_of s’ at t
annotations.comment: Paraphrase of definition: a relation between a process and an independent continuant, in which the process takes place entirely within the independent continuant

annotations.label: contains process
annotations.definition: [copied from inverse property 'occurs in'] b occurs_in c =def b is a process and c is a material entity or immaterial entity& there exists a spatiotemporal region r and b occupies_spatiotemporal_region r.& forall(t) if b exists_at t then c exists_at t & there exist spatial regions s and s’ where & b spatially_projects_onto s at t& c is occupies_spatial_region s’ at t& s is a proper_continuant_part_of s’ at t
annotations.comment: Paraphrase of definition: a relation between an independent continuant and a process, in which the process takes place entirely within the independent continuant

annotations.label: characteristic of
annotations.definition: a relation between a specifically dependent continuant (the characteristic) and any other entity (the bearer), in which the characteristic depends on the bearer for its existence.
annotations.comment: Note that this relation was previously called "inheres in", but was changed to be called "characteristic of" because BFO2 uses "inheres in" in a more restricted fashion. This relation differs from BFO2:inheres_in in two respects: (1) it does not impose a range constraint, and thus it allows qualities of processes, as well as of information entities, whereas BFO2 restricts inheres_in to only apply to independent continuants (2) it is declared functional, i.e. something can only be a characteristic of one thing.

annotations.label: has characteristic
annotations.definition: Inverse of characteristic_of

annotations.label: participates in
annotations.definition: a relation between a continuant and a process, in which the continuant is somehow involved in the process

annotations.label: has participant
annotations.definition: a relation between a process and a continuant, in which the continuant is somehow involved in the process

annotations.label: is concretized as
annotations.definition: A relationship between a generically dependent continuant and a specifically dependent continuant or process, in which the generically dependent continuant depends on some independent continuant or process in virtue of the fact that the specifically dependent continuant or process also depends on that same independent continuant. A generically dependent continuant may be concretized as multiple specifically dependent continuants or processes.

annotations.label: concretizes
annotations.definition: A relationship between a specifically dependent continuant or process and a generically dependent continuant, in which the generically dependent continuant depends on some independent continuant in virtue of the fact that the specifically dependent continuant or process also depends on that same independent continuant. Multiple specifically dependent continuants or processes can concretize the same generically dependent continuant.

annotations.label: function of
annotations.definition: a relation between a function and an independent continuant (the bearer), in which the function specifically depends on the bearer for its existence
annotations.comment: This relation is modeled after the BFO relation of the same name which was in BFO2, but is used in a more restricted sense - specifically, we model this relation as functional (inherited from characteristic-of). Note that this relation is now removed from BFO2020.

annotations.label: quality of
annotations.definition: a relation between a quality and an independent continuant (the bearer), in which the quality specifically depends on the bearer for its existence
annotations.comment: This relation is modeled after the BFO relation of the same name which was in BFO2, but is used in a more restricted sense - specifically, we model this relation as functional (inherited from characteristic-of). Note that this relation is now removed from BFO2020.

annotations.label: role of
annotations.definition: a relation between a role and an independent continuant (the bearer), in which the role specifically depends on the bearer for its existence
annotations.comment: This relation is modeled after the BFO relation of the same name which was in BFO2, but is used in a more restricted sense - specifically, we model this relation as functional (inherited from characteristic-of). Note that this relation is now removed from BFO2020.

annotations.label: has function
annotations.definition: a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a function, in which the function specifically depends on the bearer for its existence

annotations.label: has quality
annotations.definition: a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a quality, in which the quality specifically depends on the bearer for its existence

annotations.label: has role
annotations.definition: a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a role, in which the role specifically depends on the bearer for its existence

annotations.label: has disposition
annotations.definition: a relation between an independent continuant (the bearer) and a disposition, in which the disposition specifically depends on the bearer for its existence

annotations.label: disposition of
annotations.definition: inverse of has disposition
annotations.comment: This relation is modeled after the BFO relation of the same name which was in BFO2, but is used in a more restricted sense - specifically, we model this relation as functional (inherited from characteristic-of). Note that this relation is now removed from BFO2020.

annotations.label: derives from
annotations.definition: a relation between two distinct material entities, the new entity and the old entity, in which the new entity begins to exist when the old entity ceases to exist, and the new entity inherits the significant portion of the matter of the old entity

annotations.label: derives into
annotations.definition: a relation between two distinct material entities, the old entity and the new entity, in which the new entity begins to exist when the old entity ceases to exist, and the new entity inherits the significant portion of the matter of the old entity

annotations.label: location of
annotations.definition: a relation between two independent continuants, the location and the target, in which the target is entirely within the location

annotations.label: is allergic trigger for
annotations.definition: A relation between a material entity and a condition (a phenotype or disease) of a host, in which the material entity is not part of the host, and is considered harmless to non-allergic hosts, and the condition results in pathological processes that include an abnormally strong immune response against the material entity.

annotations.label: is autoimmune trigger for
annotations.definition: A relation between a material entity and a condition (a phenotype or disease) of a host, in which the material entity is part of the host itself, and the condition results in pathological processes that include an abnormally strong immune response against the material entity.

annotations.label: has allergic trigger
annotations.definition: A relation between a condition (a phenotype or disease) of a host and a material entity, in which the material entity is not part of the host, and is considered harmless to non-allergic hosts, and the condition results in pathological processes that include an abnormally strong immune response against the material entity.

annotations.label: has autoimmune trigger
annotations.definition: A relation between a condition (a phenotype or disease) of a host and a material entity, in which the material entity is part of the host itself, and the condition results in pathological processes that include an abnormally strong immune response against the material entity.

annotations.label: located in
annotations.definition: a relation between two independent continuants, the target and the location, in which the target is entirely within the location

annotations.label: 2D boundary of
annotations.definition: a relation between a 2D immaterial entity (the boundary) and a material entity, in which the boundary delimits the material entity

annotations.label: has 2D boundary
annotations.definition: a relation between a material entity and a 2D immaterial entity (the boundary), in which the boundary delimits the material entity

annotations.label: electrically_synapsed_to
annotations.definition: A relation that holds between two neurons that are electrically coupled via gap junctions.

annotations.label: tracheates
annotations.definition: The relationship that holds between a trachea or tracheole and an antomical structure that is contained in (and so provides an oxygen supply to).

annotations.label: innervated by
annotations.definition: relation between an anatomical structure and a neuron projection bundle that has synaptic terminals in the structure.

annotations.label: bounding layer of
annotations.definition:
X outer_layer_of Y iff:
. X :continuant that bearer_of some PATO:laminar
. X part_of Y
. exists Z :surface
. X has_boundary Z
. Z boundary_of Y

has_boundary: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002002
boundary_of: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002000
annotations.comment: A relationship that applies between a continuant and its outer, bounding layer.  Examples include the relationship between a multicellular organism and its integument, between an animal cell and its plasma membrane, and between a membrane bound organelle and its outer/bounding membrane.

annotations.label: coincident with
annotations.definition: A relation that holds between two linear structures that are approximately parallel to each other for their entire length and where either the two structures are adjacent to each other or one is part of the other.
annotations.comment: Example: if we define region of chromosome as any subdivision of a chromosome along its long axis, then we can define a region of chromosome that contains only gene x as 'chromosome region' that coincident_with some 'gene x', where the term gene X corresponds to a genomic sequence.

annotations.label: cell expresses
annotations.definition: A relation that applies between a cell(c) and a gene(g) , where the process of 'transcription, DNA templated (GO_0006351)' is occuring in in cell c and that process has input gene g.

annotations.label: regulates in another organism
annotations.definition: x 'regulates in other organism' y if and only if: (x is the realization of a function to exert an effect on the frequency, rate or extent of y) AND (the agents of x are produced by organism o1 and the agents of y are produced by organism o2).

annotations.label: regulates transport of
annotations.definition: A relationship that holds between a  process that regulates a transport process and the entity transported by that process.

annotations.label: occurrent part of
annotations.definition: A part of relation that applies only between occurrents.

annotations.label: has regulatory component activity
annotations.definition: A 'has regulatory component activity' B if A and B are GO molecular functions (GO_0003674), A has_component B and A is regulated by B.

annotations.label: has negative regulatory component activity
annotations.definition: A relationship that holds between a GO molecular function and a component of that molecular function that negatively regulates the activity of the whole.  More formally, A 'has regulatory component activity' B iff :A and B are GO molecular functions (GO_0003674), A has_component B and A is negatively regulated by B.
annotations.comment: By convention GO molecular functions are classified by their effector function.  Internal regulatory functions are treated as components.  For example, NMDA glutmate receptor activity is a cation channel activity with positive regulatory component 'glutamate binding' and negative regulatory components including 'zinc binding' and 'magnesium binding'.

annotations.label: has positive regulatory component activity
annotations.definition: A relationship that holds between a GO molecular function and a component of that molecular function that positively regulates the activity of the whole.  More formally, A 'has regulatory component activity' B iff :A and B are GO molecular functions (GO_0003674), A has_component B and A is positively regulated by B.
annotations.comment: By convention GO molecular functions are classified by their effector function and internal regulatory functions are treated as components.  So, for example calmodulin has a protein binding activity that has positive regulatory component activity calcium binding activity. Receptor tyrosine kinase activity is a tyrosine kinase activity that has positive regulatory component 'ligand binding'.

annotations.label: has necessary component activity
annotations.comment: A has necessary component activity B if A and B are GO molecular functions  (GO_0003674), A has_component B and B is necessary for A.  For example,  ATPase coupled transporter activity has necessary component ATPase activity; transcript factor activity has necessary component DNA binding activity.

annotations.label: has component activity
annotations.comment: A 'has component activity' B if A is A and B are molecular functions (GO_0003674) and A has_component B.

annotations.label: has component process
annotations.definition: w 'has process component' p if p and w are processes,  w 'has part' p and w is such that it can be directly disassembled into into n parts p, p2, p3, ..., pn, where these parts are of similar type.

annotations.label: has ligand
annotations.definition: A relationship that holds between between a receptor and an chemical entity, typically a small molecule or peptide, that carries information between cells or compartments of a cell and which binds the receptor and regulates its effector function.

annotations.label: transports
annotations.definition: Holds between p and c when p is a transport process or transporter activity and the outcome of this p is to move c from one location to another.

annotations.label: occurs across
annotations.definition: A relationship between a process and a barrier, where the process occurs in a region spanning the barrier. For cellular processes the barrier is typically a membrane. Examples include transport across a membrane and membrane depolarization.

annotations.label: directly regulated by
annotations.comment: Process(P2) is directly regulated by process(P1) iff: P1 regulates P2 via direct physical interaction between an agent executing P1 (or some part of P1) and an agent executing P2 (or some part of P2).  For example, if protein A has protein binding activity(P1) that targets protein B and this binding  regulates the kinase activity (P2) of protein B then P1 directly regulates P2. {xref="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-7073-9172"}

annotations.label: directly negatively regulated by
annotations.definition: Process(P2) is directly negatively regulated by process(P1) iff: P1 negatively regulates P2 via direct physical interaction between an agent executing P1 (or some part of P1) and an agent executing P2 (or some part of P2).  For example, if protein A has protein binding activity(P1) that targets protein B and this binding  negatively regulates the kinase activity (P2) of protein B then P2 directly negatively regulated by P1.

annotations.label: directly positively regulated by
annotations.definition: Process(P2) is directly postively regulated by process(P1) iff: P1 positively regulates P2 via direct physical interaction between an agent executing P1 (or some part of P1) and an agent executing P2 (or some part of P2).  For example, if protein A has protein binding activity(P1) that targets protein B and this binding positively regulates the kinase activity (P2) of protein B then P2 is directly postively regulated by P1.

annotations.label: has effector activity
annotations.definition: A 'has effector activity' B if A and B are GO molecular functions (GO_0003674),  A 'has component activity' B and B is the effector (output function) of B.  Each compound function has only one effector activity.
annotations.comment: This relation is designed for constructing compound molecular functions, typically in combination with one or more regulatory component activity relations.

annotations.label: in register with
before or simultaneous with
annotations.comment: Primitive instance level timing relation between events

annotations.label: simultaneous with
annotations.definition: x simultaneous with y iff ω(x) = ω(y) and ω(α ) = ω(α), where α is a function that maps a process to a start point, and ω is a function that maps a process to an end point and '=' indicates the same instance in time.
annotations.comment: t1 simultaneous_with t2 iff:=  t1 before_or_simultaneous_with t2  and not (t1 before t2)

annotations.label: before
annotations.comment: t1 before t2 iff:=  t1 before_or_simulataneous_with t2  and not (t1 simultaeous_with t2)

annotations.label: during which ends
annotations.comment: Previously had ID  http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002122 in test files in sandpit - but this seems to have been dropped from ro-edit.owl at some point.  No re-use under this ID AFAIK, but leaving note here in case we run in to clashes down the line.  Official ID now chosen from DOS ID range.

annotations.label: encompasses
annotations.comment: Previously had ID  http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002124 in test files in sandpit - but this seems to have been dropped from ro-edit.owl at some point.  No re-use under this ID AFAIK, but leaving note here in case we run in to clashes down the line.  Official ID now chosen from DOS ID range.

annotations.label: ends after
annotations.comment: X ends_after Y iff: end(Y) before_or_simultaneous_with end(X)

annotations.label: immediately preceded by
annotations.comment: X immediately_preceded_by Y iff: end(X) simultaneous_with start(Y)

annotations.label: during which starts
annotations.comment: Previously had ID  http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002123 in test files in sandpit - but this seems to have been dropped from ro-edit.owl at some point.  No re-use under this ID AFAIK, but leaving note here in case we run in to clashes down the line.  Official ID now chosen from DOS ID range.

annotations.label: immediately precedes
annotations.comment: X immediately_precedes_Y iff: end(X) simultaneous_with start(Y)

annotations.label: starts during
annotations.comment: X starts_during Y iff: (start(Y) before_or_simultaneous_with start(X)) AND (start(X) before_or_simultaneous_with end(Y))

annotations.label: happens during
annotations.comment: X happens_during Y iff: (start(Y) before_or_simultaneous_with start(X)) AND (end(X) before_or_simultaneous_with end(Y))

annotations.label: ends during
annotations.comment: X ends_during Y iff: ((start(Y) before_or_simultaneous_with end(X)) AND end(X) before_or_simultaneous_with end(Y).

annotations.label: has soma location
annotations.definition: Relation between a neuron and a material anatomical entity that its soma is part of.

annotations.label: fasciculates with
annotations.definition: relationship between a neuron and a neuron projection bundle (e.g.- tract or nerve bundle) that one or more of its projections travels through.

annotations.label: axon synapses in
annotations.definition: Relation between a neuron and some structure its axon forms (chemical) synapses in.

annotations.label: synapsed by
annotations.definition: Relation between an anatomical structure (including cells) and a neuron that chemically synapses to it.

annotations.label: has plasma membrane part
annotations.definition: Holds between a cell c and a protein complex or protein p if and only if that cell has as part a plasma_membrane[GO:0005886], and that plasma membrane has p as part.

annotations.label: synapsed_via_type_Ib_bouton_to
annotations.definition: A relation between a motor neuron and a muscle that it synapses to via a type Ib bouton.
annotations.comment: Expands to: has_part some ('type Ib terminal button' that has_part some ('pre-synaptic membrane' that part_of some ('synapse' that has_part some ('post-synaptic membrane' that part_of some ?Y))))

annotations.label: synapsed_via_type_Is_bouton_to
annotations.definition: A relation between a motor neuron and a muscle that it synapses to via a type Is bouton.
annotations.comment: Expands to: has_part some ('type Is terminal button' that has_part some ('pre-synaptic membrane' that part_of some ('synapse' that has_part some ('post-synaptic membrane' that part_of some ?Y))))

annotations.label: synapsed_via_type_II_bouton_to
annotations.definition: A relation between a motor neuron and a muscle that it synapses to via a type II bouton.
annotations.comment: Expands to: has_part some ('type II terminal button' that has_part some ('pre-synaptic membrane' that part_of some ('synapse' that has_part some ('post-synaptic membrane' that part_of some ?Y))))

annotations.label: synapsed_by_via_type_II_bouton
annotations.definition: Relation between a muscle and a motor neuron that synapses to it via a type II bouton.
annotations.comment: Expands to: has_part some ('presynaptic membrane' that part_of some ('synapse' that has_part some ('type II terminal button' that has_part some 'postsynaptic membrane' that part_of some ?Y)))))

annotations.label: synapsed_by_via_type_Ib_bouton
annotations.definition: Relation between a muscle and a motor neuron that synapses to it via a type Ib bouton.
annotations.comment: Expands to: has_part some ('presynaptic membrane' that part_of some ('synapse' that has_part some ('type Ib terminal button' that has_part some 'postsynaptic membrane' that part_of some ?Y)))))

annotations.label: has postsynaptic terminal in
annotations.definition: Relation between a neuron and some structure (e.g.- a brain region) in which it receives (chemical) synaptic input.

annotations.label: synapsed_by_via_type_Is_bouton
annotations.definition: Relation between a muscle and a motor neuron that synapses to it via a type Is bouton.
annotations.comment: Expands to: has_part some ('presynaptic membrane' that part_of some ('synapse' that has_part some ('type Is terminal button' that has_part some 'postsynaptic membrane' that part_of some ?Y)))))

annotations.label: has presynaptic terminal in
annotations.definition: Relation between a neuron and some structure (e.g.- a brain region) in which it receives (chemical) synaptic input.

annotations.label: synapsed_via_type_III_bouton_to
annotations.definition: A relation between a motor neuron and a muscle that it synapses to via a type III bouton.
annotations.comment: Expands to: has_part some ('type III terminal button' that has_part some ('pre-synaptic membrane' that part_of some ('synapse' that has_part some ('post-synaptic membrane' that part_of some ?Y))))

annotations.label: synapsed_by_via_type_III_bouton
annotations.definition: Relation between a muscle and a motor neuron that synapses to it via a type III bouton.
annotations.comment: Expands to: has_part some ('presynaptic membrane' that part_of some ('synapse' that has_part some ('type III terminal button' that has_part some 'postsynaptic membrane' that part_of some ?Y)))))

annotations.label: synapsed to
annotations.definition: Relation between a neuron and an anatomical structure (including cells) that it chemically synapses to.
annotations.comment:
N1 synapsed_to some N2 
Expands to:
N1 SubclassOf ( 
   has_part some (
      ‘pre-synaptic membrane ; GO:0042734’ that part_of some ( 
	‘synapse ; GO:0045202’ that has_part some (
	   ‘post-synaptic membrane ; GO:0045211’ that part_of some N2))))

annotations.label: dendrite synapsed in
annotations.definition: Relation between a neuron and some structure  (e.g.- a brain region) in which its dendrite receives synaptic input.

annotations.label: has synaptic terminal in
annotations.definition: A general relation between a neuron and some structure in which it either chemically synapses to some target or in which it receives (chemical) synaptic input.

annotations.label: overlaps
annotations.definition: x overlaps y if and only if there exists some z such that x has part z and z part of y

annotations.label: has fasciculating neuron projection
annotations.definition: The relation between a neuron projection bundle and a neuron projection that is fasciculated with it.

annotations.label: innervates
annotations.definition:
Relation between a 'neuron projection bundle' and a region in which one or more of its component neuron projections either synapses to targets or receives synaptic input.
T innervates some R
Expands_to: T has_fasciculating_neuron_projection that synapse_in some R.

annotations.label: continuous with
annotations.definition: X continuous_with Y if and only if X and Y share a fiat boundary.

annotations.label: partially overlaps
annotations.definition: x partially overlaps y iff there exists some z such that z is part of x and z is part of y, and it is also the case that neither x is part of y or y is part of x

annotations.label: derived by descent from
annotations.definition: d derived_by_descent_from a if d is specified by some genetic program that is sequence-inherited-from a genetic program that specifies a.

annotations.label: has derived by descendant
annotations.definition: inverse of derived by descent from

annotations.label: shares ancestor with
annotations.definition: two individual entities d1 and d2 stand in a shares_ancestor_with relation if and only if there exists some a such that d1 derived_by_descent_from a and d2 derived_by_descent_from a.
annotations.comment: VBO calls this homologous_to

annotations.label: only in taxon
annotations.definition: x only in taxon y if and only if x is in taxon y, and there is no other organism z such that y!=z a and x is in taxon z.

annotations.label: in taxon
annotations.definition: x is in taxon y if an only if y is an organism, and the relationship between x and y is one of: part of (reflexive), developmentally preceded by, derives from, secreted by, expressed.
annotations.comment: Connects a biological entity to its taxon of origin.

annotations.label: spatially disjoint from
annotations.definition: A is spatially_disjoint_from B if and only if they have no parts in common

annotations.label: connected to
annotations.definition: a is connected to b if and only if a and b are discrete structure, and there exists some connecting structure c, such that c connects a and b

annotations.label: connects
annotations.definition: c connects a if and only if there exist some b such that a and b are similar parts of the same system, and c connects b, specifically, c connects a with b. When one structure connects two others it unites some aspect of the function or role they play within the system.

annotations.label: attached to part of
annotations.definition: a is attached to part of b if a is attached to b, or a is attached to some p, where p is part of b.

annotations.label: supplies
annotations.definition: Relation between an arterial structure and another structure, where the arterial structure acts as a conduit channeling fluid, substance or energy.

annotations.label: drains
annotations.definition: Relation between an collecting structure and another structure, where the collecting structure acts as a conduit channeling fluid, substance or energy away from the other structure.

annotations.label: has component
annotations.definition: w 'has component' p if w 'has part' p and w is such that it can be directly disassembled into into n parts p, p2, p3, ..., pn, where these parts are of similar type.

annotations.label: has phenotype
annotations.definition: A relationship that holds between a biological entity and a phenotype. Here a phenotype is construed broadly as any kind of quality of an organism part, a collection of these qualities, or a change in quality or qualities (e.g. abnormally increased temperature). The subject of this relationship can be an organism (where the organism has the phenotype, i.e. the qualities inhere in parts of this organism), a genomic entity such as a gene or genotype (if modifications of the gene or the genotype causes the phenotype), or a condition such as a disease (such that if the condition inheres in an organism, then the organism has the phenotype).

annotations.label: phenotype of
annotations.definition: inverse of has phenotype

annotations.label: develops from
annotations.definition: x develops from y if and only if either (a) x directly develops from y or (b) there exists some z such that x directly develops from z and z develops from y
annotations.comment: This is the transitive form of the develops from relation

annotations.label: develops into
annotations.definition: inverse of develops from

annotations.label: gene product of
annotations.definition: definition "x has gene product of y if and only if y is a gene (SO:0000704) that participates in some gene expression process (GO:0010467) where the output of that process is either y or something that is ribosomally translated from x"

annotations.label: has gene product
annotations.definition: x has gene product y if and only if x is a gene (SO:0000704) that participates in some gene expression process (GO:0010467) where the output of that process is either y or something that is ribosomally translated from y

annotations.label: expressed in
annotations.definition: x expressed in y if and only if there is a gene expression process (GO:0010467) that occurs in y, and one of the following holds: (i) x is a gene, and x is transcribed into a transcript as part of the gene expression process (ii) x is a transcript, and the transcription of x is part of the gene expression process (iii) x is a mature gene product such as a protein, and x was translated or otherwise processes from a transcript that was transcribed as part of this gene expression process

annotations.label: directly develops from
annotations.definition: Candidate definition: x directly_develops from y if and only if there exists some developmental process (GO:0032502) p such that x and y both participate in p, and x is the output of p and y is the input of p, and a substantial portion of the matter of x comes from y, and the start of x is coincident with or after the end of y.
annotations.comment: TODO - add child relations from DOS

annotations.label: parasitoid of
annotations.definition: A parasite that kills or sterilizes its host

annotations.label: has parasitoid
annotations.definition: inverse of parasitoid of

annotations.label: directly develops into
annotations.definition: inverse of directly develops from

annotations.label: regulates
annotations.definition: p regulates q iff p is causally upstream of q, the execution of p is not constant and varies according to specific conditions, and p influences the rate or magnitude of execution of q due to an effect either on some enabler of q or some enabler of a part of q.

annotations.label: negatively regulates
annotations.definition: p negatively regulates q iff p regulates q, and p decreases the rate or magnitude of execution of q.

annotations.label: positively regulates
annotations.definition: p positively regulates q iff p regulates q, and p increases the rate or magnitude of execution of q.

annotations.label: has prototype
annotations.definition: x has prototype y if and only if x is an instance of C and y is a prototypical instance of C. For example, every instance of heart, both normal and abnormal is related by the has prototype relation to some instance of a "canonical" heart, which participates in blood circulation.

annotations.label: capable of
annotations.definition: A relation between a material entity (such as a cell) and a process, in which the material entity has the ability to carry out the process.

annotations.label: capable of part of
annotations.definition: c stands in this relationship to p if and only if there exists some p' such that c is capable_of p', and p' is part_of p.

annotations.label: surrounded by
annotations.definition: x surrounded_by y if and only if (1) x is adjacent to y and for every region r that is adjacent to x, r overlaps y (2) the shared boundary between x and y occupies the majority of the outermost boundary of x

annotations.label: adjacent to
annotations.definition: x adjacent to y if and only if x and y share a boundary.

annotations.label: surrounds
annotations.definition: inverse of surrounded by

annotations.label: temporally related to
annotations.comment: A relation that holds between two occurrents. This is a grouping relation that collects together all the Allen relations.

annotations.label: starts
annotations.definition: inverse of starts with

annotations.label: starts with
annotations.definition: x starts with y if and only if x has part y and the time point at which x starts is equivalent to the time point at which y starts. Formally: α(y) = α(x) ∧ ω(y) < ω(x), where α is a function that maps a process to a start point, and ω is a function that maps a process to an end point.

annotations.label: develops from part of
annotations.definition: x develops from part of y if and only if there exists some z such that x develops from z and z is part of y

annotations.label: develops in
annotations.definition: x develops_in y if x is located in y whilst x is developing

annotations.label: obligate parasite of
annotations.definition: A sub-relation of parasite-of in which the parasite that cannot complete its life cycle without a host.

annotations.label: facultative parasite of
annotations.definition: A sub-relations of parasite-of in which the parasite that can complete its life cycle independent of a host.

annotations.label: ends
annotations.definition: inverse of ends with

annotations.label: ends with
annotations.definition: x ends with y if and only if x has part y and the time point at which x ends is equivalent to the time point at which y ends. Formally: α(y) > α(x) ∧ ω(y) = ω(x), where α is a function that maps a process to a start point, and ω is a function that maps a process to an end point.

annotations.label: has start location
annotations.definition: x 'has starts location' y if and only if there exists some process z such that x 'starts with' z and z 'occurs in' y

annotations.label: has end location
annotations.definition: x 'has end location' y if and only if there exists some process z such that x 'ends with' z and z 'occurs in' y

annotations.label: has input
annotations.definition: p has input c iff: p is a process, c is a material entity, c is a participant in p, c is present at the start of p, and the state of c is modified during p.

annotations.label: has output
annotations.definition: p has output c iff c is a participant in p, c is present at the end of p, and c is not present in the same state at the beginning of p.

annotations.label: stem parasite of
annotations.definition: A parasite-of relationship in which the host is a plant and the parasite that attaches to the host stem (PO:0009047)

annotations.label: root parasite of
annotations.definition: A parasite-of relationship in which the host is a plant and the parasite that attaches to the host root (PO:0009005)

annotations.label: hemiparasite of
annotations.definition: A sub-relation of parasite-of in which the parasite is a plant, and the parasite is parasitic under natural conditions and is also photosynthetic to some degree. Hemiparasites may just obtain water and mineral nutrients from the host plant. Many obtain at least part of their organic nutrients from the host as well.

annotations.label: has component participant
annotations.definition: X 'has component participant' Y means X 'has participant' Y and there is a cardinality constraint that specifies the numbers of Ys.

annotations.label: has exposure receptor
annotations.definition: A broad relationship between an exposure event or process and any entity (e.g., an organism, organism population, or an organism part) that interacts with an exposure stimulus during the exposure event.

annotations.label: has exposure stressor
annotations.definition: A broad relationship between an exposure event or process and any agent, stimulus, activity, or event that causes stress or tension on an organism and interacts with an exposure receptor during an exposure event.

annotations.label: has exposure route
annotations.definition: A broad relationship between an exposure event or process and a process by which the exposure stressor comes into contact with the exposure receptor

annotations.label: has exposure transport path
annotations.definition: A broad relationship between an exposure event or process and the course takes from the source to the target.

annotations.label: related via exposure to
annotations.definition: Any relationship between an exposure event or process and any other entity.

annotations.label: over-expressed in
annotations.definition: g is over-expressed in t iff g is expressed in t, and the expression level of g is increased relative to some background.

annotations.label: under-expressed in
annotations.definition: g is under-expressed in t iff g is expressed in t, and the expression level of g is decreased relative to some background.

annotations.label: has active ingredient
annotations.definition: A relationship that holds between a substance and a chemical entity, if the chemical entity is part of the substance, and the chemical entity forms the biologically active component of the substance.

annotations.label: active ingredient in
annotations.definition: inverse of has active ingredient

annotations.label: connecting branch of
annotations.definition: b connecting-branch-of s iff b is connected to s, and there exists some tree-like structure t such that the mereological sum of b plus s is either the same as t or a branching-part-of t.

annotations.label: has connecting branch
annotations.definition: inverse of connecting branch of

annotations.label: has developmental contribution from
annotations.definition: x has developmental contribution from y iff x has some part z such that z develops from y

annotations.label: developmentally contributes to
annotations.definition: inverse of has developmental contribution from

annotations.label: developmentally induced by
annotations.definition: t1 induced_by t2 if there is a process of developmental induction (GO:0031128) with t1 and t2 as interacting participants. t2 causes t1 to change its fate from a precursor material anatomical entity type T to T', where T' develops_from T

annotations.label: developmentally induces
annotations.definition: Inverse of developmentally induced by

annotations.label: developmentally preceded by
annotations.definition: Candidate definition: x developmentally related to y if and only if there exists some developmental process (GO:0032502) p such that x and y both participates in p, and x is the output of p and y is the input of p
annotations.comment: This relation groups together various other developmental relations. It is fairly generic, encompassing induction, developmental contribution and direct and transitive develops from

annotations.label: has biological role
annotations.definition: c has-biological-role r iff c has-role r and r is a biological role (CHEBI:24432)

annotations.label: has application role
annotations.definition: c has-application-role r iff c has-role r and r is an application role (CHEBI:33232)

annotations.label: has chemical role
annotations.definition: c has-chemical-role r iff c has-role r and r is a chemical role (CHEBI:51086)

annotations.label: acts upstream of
annotations.definition: c acts upstream of p if and only if c enables some f that is involved in p' and p' occurs chronologically before p, is not part of p, and affects the execution of p. c is a material entity and f, p, p' are processes.

annotations.label: acts upstream of or within
annotations.definition: c acts upstream of or within p if c is enables f, and f is causally upstream of or within p. c is a material entity and p is an process.

annotations.label: developmentally replaces
annotations.definition: x developmentally replaces y if and only if there is some developmental process that causes x to move or to cease to exist, and for the site that was occupied by x to become occupied by y, where y either comes into existence in this site or moves to this site from somewhere else

annotations.label: developmentally succeeded by
annotations.definition: Inverse of developmentally preceded by

annotations.label: ubiquitously expressed in
annotations.definition: x is ubiquitously expressed in y if and only if x is expressed in y, and the majority of cells in y express x

annotations.label: expresses
annotations.definition: y expresses x if and only if there is a gene expression process (GO:0010467) that occurs in y, and one of the following holds: (i) x is a gene, and x is transcribed into a transcript as part of the gene expression process (ii) x is a transcript, and x was transcribed from a gene as part of the gene expression process (iii) x is a mature gene product (protein or RNA), and x was translated or otherwise processed from a transcript that was transcribed as part of the gene expression process.

annotations.label: ubiquitously expresses
annotations.definition: inverse of ubiquiotously expressed in

annotations.label: results in developmental progression of
annotations.definition: p results in the developmental progression of s iff p is a developmental process and s is an anatomical entity and p causes s to undergo a change in state at some point along its natural developmental cycle (this cycle starts with its formation, through the mature structure, and ends with its loss).

annotations.label: results in development of
annotations.definition: p 'results in development of' c if and only if p is a developmental process and p results in the state of c changing from its initial state as a primordium or anlage through its mature state and to its final state.

annotations.label: results in formation of anatomical entity
results in morphogenesis of
annotations.definition: The relationship that links an entity with the process that results in the formation and shaping of that entity over time from an immature to a mature state.

annotations.label: results in maturation of
annotations.definition: The relationship that links an entity with a process that results in the progression of the entity over time that is independent of changes in it's shape and results in an end point state of that entity.

annotations.label: is treated by substance
annotations.definition: Inverse of 'is substance that treats'

annotations.label: has habitat
annotations.definition: x 'has habitat' y if and only if: x is an organism, y is a habitat, and y can sustain and allow the growth of a population of xs.
annotations.comment: A population of xs will possess adaptations (either evolved naturally or via artifical selection) which permit it to exist and grow in y.

annotations.label: causally upstream of, positive effect
annotations.definition: p is causally upstream of, positive effect q iff p is casually upstream of q, and the execution of p is required for the execution of q.
annotations.comment: holds between x and y if and only if x is causally upstream of y and the progression of x increases the frequency, rate or extent of y

annotations.label: causally upstream of, negative effect
annotations.definition: p is causally upstream of, negative effect q iff p is casually upstream of q, and the execution of p decreases the execution of q.

annotations.label: has exposure stimulus
annotations.definition: A relationship between an exposure event or process and any agent, stimulus, activity, or event that causally effects an organism and interacts with an exposure receptor during an exposure event.

annotations.label: transports or maintains localization of
annotations.definition: Holds between p and c when p is a localization process (localization covers maintenance of localization as well as its establishment)  and the outcome of this process is to regulate the localization of c.

annotations.label: characteristic of part of
annotations.definition: q characteristic of part of w if and only if there exists some p such that q inheres in p and p part of w.

annotations.label: results in acquisition of features of
annotations.definition: The relationship that links a specified entity with the process that results in an unspecified entity acquiring the features and characteristics of the specified entity

annotations.label: evolutionarily related to
annotations.definition: A relationship that holds via some environmental process

annotations.label: ecologically related to
annotations.definition: A relationship that is mediated in some way by the environment or environmental feature (ENVO:00002297)

annotations.label: mereotopologically related to
annotations.definition: A mereological relationship or a topological relationship

annotations.label: developmentally related to
annotations.definition: A relationship that holds between entities participating in some developmental process (GO:0032502)

annotations.label: colocalizes with
annotations.definition: a colocalizes_with b if and only if  a is transiently or peripherally associated with b[GO].

annotations.label: enables
annotations.definition: c enables p iff c is capable of p and c acts to execute p.

annotations.label: functionally related to
annotations.definition: A grouping relationship for any relationship directly involving a function, or that holds because of a function of one of the related entities.

annotations.label: part of structure that is capable of
annotations.definition: this relation holds between c and p when c is part of some c', and c' is capable of p.

annotations.label: genomically related to
annotations.definition: holds between two entities when some genome-level process such as gene expression is involved. This includes transcriptional, spliceosomal events. These relations can be used between either macromolecule entities (such as regions of nucleic acid) or between their abstract informational counterparts.

annotations.label: involved in
annotations.definition: c involved_in p if and only if c enables some process p', and p' is part of p

annotations.label: regulates levels of
annotations.definition: p regulates levels of c if p regulates some amount (PATO:0000070) of c

annotations.label: enabled by
annotations.definition: inverse of enables

annotations.label: regulated by
annotations.definition: inverse of regulates

annotations.label: negatively regulated by
annotations.definition: inverse of negatively regulates

annotations.label: positively regulated by
annotations.definition: inverse of positively regulates

annotations.label: related via localization to
annotations.definition: A relationship that holds via some process of localization

annotations.label: has target start location
annotations.definition: This relationship holds between p and l when p is a transport or localization process in which the outcome is to move some cargo c from some initial location l to some destination.

annotations.label: has target end location
annotations.definition: This relationship holds between p and l when p is a transport or localization process in which the outcome is to move some cargo c from a an initial location to some destination l.

annotations.label: imports
annotations.definition: Holds between p and c when p is a transportation or localization process and the outcome of this process is to move c to a destination that is part of some s, where the start location of c is part of the region that surrounds s.

annotations.label: results in transport along
annotations.definition: Holds between p and l when p is a transportation or localization process and the outcome of this process is to move c from one location to another, and the route taken by c follows a path that is aligned_with l

annotations.label: results in transport across
annotations.definition: Holds between p and m when p is a transportation or localization process and the outcome of this process is to move c from one location to another, and the route taken by c follows a path that crosses m.

annotations.label: results in transport to from or in
exports
annotations.definition: Holds between p and c when p is a transportation or localization process and the outcome of this process is to move c to a destination that is part of some s, where the end location of c is part of the region that surrounds s.

annotations.label: results in commitment to
annotations.definition: p 'results in commitment to' c if and only if p is a developmental process and c is a cell and p results in the state of c changing such that is can only develop into a single cell type.

annotations.label: results in determination of
annotations.definition: p 'results in determination of' c if and only if p is a developmental process and c is a cell and p results in the state of c changing to be determined. Once a cell becomes determined, it becomes committed to differentiate down a particular pathway regardless of its environment.

annotations.label: member of
annotations.definition: is member of is a mereological relation between a item and a collection.

annotations.label: has member
annotations.definition: has member is a mereological relation between a collection and an item.

annotations.label: input of
annotations.definition: inverse of has input

annotations.label: output of
annotations.definition: inverse of has output

annotations.label: results in structural organization of
annotations.definition: A relationship between a process and an anatomical entity such that the process contributes to the act of creating the structural organization of the anatomical entity.

annotations.label: results in specification of
annotations.definition: The relationship linking a cell and its participation in a process that results in the fate of the cell being specified. Once specification has taken place, a cell will be committed to differentiate down a specific pathway if left in its normal environment.

annotations.label: results in developmental induction of
annotations.definition: p results in developmental induction of c if and only if p is a collection of cell-cell signaling processes that signal to a neighbouring tissue that is the precursor of the mature c, where the signaling results in the commitment to cell types necessary for the formation of c.

annotations.label: attached to
annotations.definition: a is attached to b if and only if a and b are discrete objects or object parts, and there are physical connections between a and b such that a force pulling a will move b, or a force pulling b will move a

annotations.label: has muscle origin
annotations.definition: m has_muscle_origin s iff m is attached_to s, and it is the case that when m contracts, s does not move. The site of the origin tends to be more proximal and have greater mass than what the other end attaches to.

annotations.label: has muscle insertion
annotations.definition: m has_muscle_insertion s iff m is attaches_to s, and it is the case that when m contracts, s moves. Insertions are usually connections of muscle via tendon to bone.

annotations.label: derived from ancestral fusion of
annotations.definition: x has_fused_element y iff: there exists some z : x has_part z, z homologous_to y, and y is a distinct element, the boundary between x and z is largely fiat
annotations.comment: A has_fused_element B does not imply that A has_part some B: rather than A has_part some B', where B' that has some evolutionary relationship to B.

annotations.label: in branching relationship with
annotations.definition: A relationship that holds between two material entities in a system of connected structures, where the branching relationship holds based on properties of the connecting network.

annotations.label: tributary of
annotations.definition: x tributary_of y if and only if x a channel for the flow of a substance into y, where y is larger than x. If x and y are hydrographic features, then y is the main stem of a river, or a lake or bay, but not the sea or ocean. If x and y are anatomical, then y is a vein.

annotations.label: distributary of
annotations.definition: x distributary_of y if and only if x is capable of channeling the flow of a substance to y, where y channels less of the substance than x
annotations.comment: This is both a mereotopological relationship and a relationship defined in connection to processes. It concerns both the connecting structure, and how this structure is disposed to causally affect flow processes

annotations.label: anabranch of
annotations.definition: x anabranch_of y if x is a distributary of y (i.e. it channels a from a larger flow from y) and x ultimately channels the flow back into y.

annotations.label: spatially coextensive with
annotations.definition: x spatially_coextensive_with y if and inly if x and y have the same location

annotations.label: branching part of
annotations.definition: x is a branching part of y if and only if x is part of y and x is connected directly or indirectly to the main stem of y

annotations.label: main stem of
annotations.definition: x main_stem_of y if y is a branching structure and x is a channel that traces a linear path through y, such that x has higher capacity than any other such path.

annotations.label: proper distributary of
annotations.definition: x proper_distributary_of y iff x distributary_of y and x does not flow back into y

annotations.label: proper tributary of
annotations.definition: x proper_tributary_of y iff x tributary_of y and x does not originate from y

annotations.label: has developmental potential involving
annotations.definition: x has developmental potential involving y iff x is capable of a developmental process with output y. y may be the successor of x, or may be a different structure in the vicinity (as for example in the case of developmental induction).

annotations.label: has potential to developmentally contribute to
annotations.definition: x has potential to developmentrally contribute to y iff x developmentally contributes to y or x is capable of developmentally contributing to y

annotations.label: has potential to developmentally induce
annotations.definition: x has potential to developmentally induce y iff x developmentally induces y or x is capable of developmentally inducing y

annotations.label: has potential to develop into
annotations.definition: x has the potential to develop into y iff x develops into y or if x is capable of developing into y
annotations.comment: This relation has a stronger meaning than merely indicating that X may develop into Y, in that the capability of developing into Y is always present, even if X does not always actually develop into Y. In particular, this means that if Y is restricted to a given taxon T, X is necessarily likewise restricted.

annotations.label: has potential to directly develop into
annotations.definition: x has potential to directly develop into y iff x directly develops into y or x is capable of directly developing into y

annotations.label: has direct input
annotations.definition: p has direct input c iff c is a participant in p, c is present at the start of p, and the state of c is modified during p.

annotations.label: causally downstream of
annotations.definition: inverse of upstream of

annotations.label: indirectly positively regulates
annotations.definition: p indirectly positively regulates q iff p is indirectly causally upstream of q and p positively regulates q.

annotations.label: indirectly negatively regulates
annotations.definition: p indirectly negatively regulates q iff p is indirectly causally upstream of q and p negatively regulates q.

annotations.label: causally related to
annotations.definition: relation that links two events, processes, states, or objects such that one event, process, state, or object (a cause) contributes to the production of another event, process, state, or object (an effect) where the cause is partly or wholly responsible for the effect, and the effect is partly or wholly dependent on the cause.

annotations.label: causally upstream of
annotations.definition: p is causally upstream of q iff p is causally related to q, the end of p precedes the end of q, and p is not an occurrent part of q.

annotations.label: immediately causally upstream of
annotations.definition: p is immediately causally upstream of q iff p is causally upstream of q, and the end of p is coincident with the beginning of q.

annotations.label: provides input for
annotations.definition: p provides input for q iff p is immediately causally upstream of q, and there exists some c such that p has_output c and q has_input c.

annotations.label: transitively provides input for
annotations.definition: transitive form of directly_provides_input_for

annotations.label: causally upstream of or within
annotations.definition: p is 'causally upstream or within' q iff p is causally related to q, and the end of p precedes, or is coincident with, the end of q.

annotations.label: differs in
annotations.comment: This is an exploratory relation

annotations.label: causally downstream of or within
annotations.definition: inverse of causally upstream of or within

annotations.label: involved in regulation of
annotations.definition: c involved in regulation of p if c is involved in some p' and p' regulates some p

annotations.label: involved in positive regulation of
annotations.definition: c involved in regulation of p if c is involved in some p' and p' positively regulates some p

annotations.label: involved in negative regulation of
annotations.definition: c involved in regulation of p if c is involved in some p' and p' negatively regulates some p

annotations.label: involved in or involved in regulation of
annotations.definition: c involved in or regulates p if and only if either (i) c is involved in p or (ii) c is involved in regulation of p

annotations.label: is active in
annotations.definition: c executes activity in d if and only if c enables p and p occurs_in d.  Assuming no action at a distance by gene products, if a gene product enables (is capable of) a process that occurs in some structure, it must have at least some part in that structure.

annotations.label: contributes to morphology of
annotations.definition: p contributes to morphology of w if and only if a change in the morphology of p entails a change in the morphology of w. Examples: every skull contributes to morphology of the head which it is a part of. Counter-example: nuclei do not generally contribute to the morphology of the cell they are part of, as they are buffered by cytoplasm.

annotations.label: interacts with
annotations.definition: A relationship that holds between two entities in which the processes executed by the two entities are causally connected.

annotations.label: molecularly interacts with
annotations.definition: An interaction relationship in which the two partners are molecular entities that directly physically interact with each other for example via a stable binding interaction or a brief interaction during which one modifies the other.

annotations.label: biotically interacts with
annotations.definition: An interaction relationship in which at least one of the partners is an organism and the other is either an organism or an abiotic entity with which the organism interacts.

annotations.label: trophically interacts with
annotations.definition: An interaction relationship in which the partners are related via a feeding relationship.

annotations.label: preys on
annotations.definition: An interaction relationship involving a predation process, where the subject kills the target in order to eat it or to feed to siblings, offspring or group members

annotations.label: symbiotically interacts with
annotations.definition: A biotic interaction in which the two organisms live together in more or less intimate association.

annotations.label: commensually interacts with
annotations.definition: An interaction relationship between two organisms living together in more or less intimate association in a relationship in which one benefits and the other is unaffected (GO).

annotations.label: mutualistically interacts with
annotations.definition: An interaction relationship between two organisms living together in more or less intimate association in a relationship in which both organisms benefit from each other (GO).

annotations.label: interacts with via parasite-host interaction
annotations.definition: An interaction relationship between two organisms living together in more or less intimate association in a relationship in which association is disadvantageous or destructive to one of the organisms (GO).

annotations.label: parasite of
annotations.definition: A parasite-host relationship where an organism benefits at the expense of another.

annotations.label: parasitized by
annotations.definition: Inverse of parasite of

annotations.label: participates in a abiotic-biotic interaction with
annotations.definition: A biotic interaction relationship in which one partner is an organism and the other partner is inorganic. For example, the relationship between a sponge and the substrate to which is it anchored.

annotations.label: directly regulates activity of
annotations.definition:
The entity A, immediately upstream of the entity B, has an activity that regulates an activity performed by B. For example, A and B may be gene products and binding of B by A regulates the kinase activity of B.

A and B can be physically interacting but not necessarily. Immediately upstream means there are no intermediate entity between A and B.

annotations.label: directly negatively regulates activity of
annotations.definition:
The entity A, immediately upstream of the entity B, has an activity that negatively regulates an activity performed by B. 
For example, A and B may be gene products and binding of B by A negatively regulates the kinase activity of B.

annotations.label: directly positively regulates activity of
annotations.definition:
The entity A, immediately upstream of the entity B, has an activity that positively regulates an activity performed by B. 
For example, A and B may be gene products and binding of B by A positively regulates the kinase activity of B.

annotations.label: transmitted by
annotations.definition: A relationship that holds between a disease and organism

annotations.label: has symptom
annotations.definition: A relation that holds between a disease or an organism and a phenotype

annotations.label: has host
annotations.definition: X 'has host' y if and only if: x is an organism, y is an organism, and x can live on the surface of or within the body of y

annotations.label: acquires nutrients from
annotations.definition: Inverse of provides nutrients for

annotations.label: preyed upon by
annotations.definition: inverse of preys on

annotations.label: helper property (not for use in curation)
provides nutrients for
annotations.definition: A biotic interaction where a material entity provides nutrition for an organism.

annotations.label: eats
annotations.definition: A biotic interaction where one organism consumes a material entity through a type of mouth or other oral opening.

annotations.label: is eaten by
annotations.definition: Inverse of eats

annotations.label: is evidence for
annotations.definition:
A relationship between a piece of evidence a and some entity b, where b is  an information content entity, material entity or process, and 
the a supports either the existence of b, or the truth value of b.

annotations.label: composed primarily of
annotations.definition: x composed_primarily_of y if and only if more than half of the mass of x is made from y or units of the same type as y.

annotations.label: child nucleus of
annotations.definition: c is a child nucleus of d if and only if c and d are both nuclei and parts of cells c' and d', where c' is derived from d' by mitosis and the genetic material in c is a copy of the generic material in d

annotations.label: child nucleus of in hermaphrodite
annotations.definition: A child nucleus relationship in which the cells are part of a hermaphroditic organism

annotations.label: child nucleus of in male
annotations.definition: A child nucleus relationship in which the cells are part of a male organism

annotations.label: has part that occurs in
annotations.definition: p has part that occurs in c if and only if there exists some p1, such that p has_part p1, and p1 occurs in c.

annotations.label: ubiquitinates
annotations.definition: An interaction relation between x and y in which x catalyzes a reaction in which one or more ubiquitin groups are added to y

annotations.label: relation between physical entity and a process or stage
existence starts during
annotations.definition: x existence starts during y if and only if the time point at which x starts is after or equivalent to the time point at which y starts and before or equivalent to the time point at which y ends. Formally: x existence starts during y iff α(x) >= α(y) & α(x) <= ω(y).

annotations.label: existence starts with
annotations.definition: x starts ends with y if and only if the time point at which x starts is equivalent to the time point at which y starts. Formally: x existence starts with y iff α(x) = α(y).

annotations.label: existence overlaps
annotations.definition: x existence overlaps y if and only if either (a) the start of x is part of y or (b) the end of x is part of y. Formally: x existence starts and ends during y iff (α(x) >= α(y) & α(x) <= ω(y)) OR (ω(x) <= ω(y) & ω(x) >= α(y))
annotations.comment: The relations here were created based on work originally by Fabian Neuhaus and David Osumi-Sutherland. The work has not yet been vetted and errors in definitions may have occurred during transcription.

annotations.label: existence starts and ends during
annotations.definition: x exists during y if and only if: 1) the time point at which x begins to exist is after or equal to the time point at which y begins and 2) the time point at which x ceases to exist is before or equal to the point at which y ends. Formally: x existence starts and ends during y iff α(x) >= α(y) & α(x) <= ω(y) & ω(x) <= ω(y) & ω(x) >= α(y)
annotations.comment: The relations here were created based on work originally by Fabian Neuhaus and David Osumi-Sutherland. The work has not yet been vetted and errors in definitions may have occurred during transcription.

annotations.label: existence ends during
annotations.definition: x existence ends during y if and only if the time point at which x ends is before or equivalent to the time point at which y ends and after or equivalent to the point at which y starts. Formally: x existence ends during y iff ω(x) <= ω(y) and ω(x) >= α(y).
annotations.comment: The relations here were created based on work originally by Fabian Neuhaus and David Osumi-Sutherland. The work has not yet been vetted and errors in definitions may have occurred during transcription.

annotations.label: existence ends with
annotations.definition: x existence ends with y if and only if the time point at which x ends is equivalent to the time point at which y ends. Formally: x existence ends with y iff ω(x) = ω(y).
annotations.comment: The relations here were created based on work originally by Fabian Neuhaus and David Osumi-Sutherland. The work has not yet been vetted and errors in definitions may have occurred during transcription.

annotations.label: transformation of
annotations.definition: x transformation of y if x is the immediate transformation of y, or is linked to y through a chain of transformation relationships

annotations.label: immediate transformation of
annotations.definition: x immediate transformation of y iff x immediately succeeds y temporally at a time boundary t, and all of the matter present in x at t is present in y at t, and all the matter in y at t is present in x at t

annotations.label: existence starts during or after
annotations.definition: x existence starts during or after y if and only if the time point at which x starts is after or equivalent to the time point at which y starts. Formally: x existence starts during or after y iff α (x) >= α (y).
annotations.comment: The relations here were created based on work originally by Fabian Neuhaus and David Osumi-Sutherland. The work has not yet been vetted and errors in definitions may have occurred during transcription.

annotations.label: existence ends during or before
annotations.definition: x existence ends during or before y if and only if the time point at which x ends is before or equivalent to the time point at which y ends.
annotations.comment: The relations here were created based on work originally by Fabian Neuhaus and David Osumi-Sutherland. The work has not yet been vetted and errors in definitions may have occurred during transcription.

annotations.label: causal agent in process
annotations.definition: A relationship between a material entity and a process where the material entity has some causal role that influences the process

annotations.label: causal relation between processes
annotations.definition: p is causally related to q if and only if p or any part of p and q or any part of q are linked by a chain of events where each event pair is one where the execution of p influences the execution of q. p may be upstream, downstream, part of, or a container of q.

annotations.label: towards
annotations.definition: q towards e2 if and only if q is a relational quality such that q inheres-in some e, and e != e2 and q is dependent on e2

annotations.label: has intermediate
annotations.definition: p has intermediate c if and only if p has parts p1, p2 and p1 has output c, and p2 has input c

annotations.label: determined by
annotations.definition: s determined by f if and only if s is a type of system, and f is a material entity that is part of s, such that f exerts a strong causal influence on the functioning of s, and the removal of f would cause the collapse of s.

annotations.label: determines
annotations.definition: inverse of determined by

annotations.label: determined by part of
annotations.definition: s 'determined by part of' w if and only if there exists some f such that (1) s 'determined by' f and (2) f part_of w, or f=w.

annotations.label: transcribed from
annotations.definition: x is transcribed from y if and only if x is synthesized from template y

annotations.label: transcribed to
annotations.definition: inverse of transcribed from

annotations.label: ribosomal translation of
annotations.definition: x is the ribosomal translation of y if and only if a ribosome reads x through a series of triplet codon-amino acid adaptor activities (GO:0030533) and produces y

annotations.label: ribosomally translates to
annotations.definition: inverse of ribosomal translation of

annotations.label: sequentially related to
annotations.definition: A relation that holds between two entities that have the property of being sequences or having sequences.

annotations.label: sequentially adjacent to
annotations.definition: x is sequentially adjacent to y iff x and y do not overlap and if there are no base units intervening between x and y

annotations.label: has start sequence
annotations.definition: x has start sequence y if the start of x is identical to the start of y, and x has y as a subsequence

annotations.label: is start sequence of
annotations.definition: inverse of has start sequence

annotations.label: has end sequence
annotations.definition: x has end sequence y if the end of x is identical to the end of y, and x has y as a subsequence

annotations.label: is end sequence of
annotations.definition: inverse of has end sequence

annotations.label: is consecutive sequence of
annotations.definition: x is a consecutive sequence of y iff x has subsequence y, and all the parts of x are made of zero or more repetitions of y or sequences as the same type as y.

annotations.label: is sequentially aligned with
annotations.definition: x is sequentially aligned with if a significant portion bases of x and y correspond in terms of their base type and their relative ordering

annotations.label: bounds sequence of
annotations.definition: x bounds the sequence of y iff the upstream-most part of x is upstream of or coincident with the upstream-most part of y, and the downstream-most part of x is downstream of or coincident with the downstream-most part of y

annotations.label: is bound by sequence of
annotations.definition: inverse of bounds sequence of

annotations.label: has subsequence
annotations.definition: x has subsequence y iff all of the sequence parts of y are sequence parts of x

annotations.label: is subsequence of
annotations.definition: inverse of has subsequence

annotations.label: overlaps sequence of
annotations.definition: x overlaps the sequence of y if and only if x has a subsequence z and z is a subsequence of y.

annotations.label: does not overlap sequence of
annotations.definition: x does not overlap the sequence of y if and only if there is no z such that x has a subsequence z and z is a subsequence of y.

annotations.label: is upstream of sequence of
annotations.definition: inverse of downstream of sequence of

annotations.label: is downstream of sequence of
annotations.definition: x is downstream of the sequence of y iff either (1) x and y have sequence units, and all units of x are downstream of all units of y, or (2) x and y are sequence units, and x is either immediately downstream of y, or transitively downstream of y.

annotations.label: is immediately downstream of sequence of
annotations.definition: x is immediately downstream of the sequence of y iff either (1) x and y have sequence units, and all units of x are downstream of all units of y, and x is sequentially adjacent to y, or (2) x and y are sequence units, in which case the immediately downstream relation is primitive and defined by context: for DNA bases, y would be adjacent and 5' to y

annotations.label: is immediately upstream of sequence of
annotations.definition: inverse of immediately downstream of

annotations.label: has skeleton
annotations.definition: A relation between a segment or subdivision of an organism and the maximal subdivision of material entities that provides structural support for that segment or subdivision.

annotations.label: results in ending of
annotations.definition: p results in the end of s if p results in a change of state in s whereby s either ceases to exist, or s becomes functionally impaired or s has its fate committed such that it is put on a path to be degraded.

annotations.label: hyperparasite of
annotations.definition: x is a hyperparasite of y iff x is a parasite of a parasite of the target organism y

annotations.label: hyperparasitized by
annotations.definition: inverse of hyperparasite of

annotations.label: allelopath of
annotations.definition: A relationship between organisms where one organism is influenced by the biochemicals produced by another.
annotations.comment: Allelopathy is a phenomenon in which one organism releases chemicals to positively or negatively influence the growth, survival or reproduction of other organisms in its vicinity.

annotations.label: pathogen of
annotations.definition: Inverse of has pathogen
